title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Quincy Gas owner Harvey Kertzman seeks a violist to perform for customers filling up at his station.
A retired violist has agreed to begin performances as weather warms, but Kertzman still seeks more musicians.
Signs remain posted at the gas station, inviting violists to apply for the unique gig in Quincy, MA.

title: Report: Patriots agree to sign edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots agree to sign Dre'Mont Jones to a three-year deal worth up to $39.5 million.
Jones recorded a career-high seven sacks and 24 QB hits last season with the Ravens.
Jones has played for four teams, including the Broncos, and brings experience to Patriots' pass rush.

title: How a Boston Marathon runner finds purpose in mentoring and miles
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Victoria Walker, 31, of Brighton, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Minds Matter Boston.
Walker mentors two 16-year-olds with Minds Matter Boston, helping them gain confidence for college applications.
Running provides Walker a supportive community and purpose, merging her passion for mentoring and running.

title: Meet the New England Aquarium’s viral seal and his rubber duckie pal
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Reggae, a harbor seal at the New England Aquarium, went viral for playing with a rubber duckie.
The viral video is the aquarium's second-best Instagram post, with nearly 500,000 views and 100,000 engagements.
Enrichment activities like the 'Find It' game highlight harbor seals' intelligence and curiosity, say trainers.

title: What we know about Bravo’s ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 at 9 p.m. on Bravo.
The show will feature local personalities including a former reporter, pizzeria co-owner, and a local cannabis business owner.
Fenway Park will host a Bravo night with the RHORI cast on April 17 during the Red Sox game against the Tigers.

title: 24 Boston businesses approved for new or upgraded liquor licenses
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Boston approved 24 new or upgraded liquor licenses for restaurants, part of state legislation adding 225 licenses since 2024.
13 businesses received licenses from Boston's Licensing Board, targeting neighborhoods like East Boston, Roslindale, and Roxbury.
Six restaurants upgraded from beer-and-wine to all-alcohol licenses, influenced by various legislative changes and public need assessments.

title: Mass. man nabbed after allegedly driving over 100 mph in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 21-year-old Attleboro man was arrested for driving 104 mph in a 55 mph zone on Route 202 in Rindge.
Rindge Police stopped the vehicle, a 2020 Kia Stinger, shortly after 11 p.m. following reports of excessive speed.
The man faces charges of reckless operation and is scheduled for an April 5 arraignment, risking fines and a license suspension.

title: AirTag leads volunteers to hidden cat and six kittens in Waltham
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Volunteers from The Cat Connection used an AirTag to locate a cat and her six kittens in a Waltham storage site.
Emerald, a stray cat, led rescuers to her litter in debris amidst a New England blizzard after being released.
The rescued cat family will be kept in a foster home and available for adoption in two months.

title: N.H. Senate approves toll hike for out-of-state drivers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: N.H. Senate approved a toll hike proposal for out-of-state drivers to fund infrastructure improvements, despite Governor Ayotte's opposition.
The toll increase could generate $53.3 million in revenue in fiscal year 2027, rising to $81.4 million by fiscal year 2036.
Toll rates would increase by $0.50 to $1 at several plazas, affecting drivers without a N.H. E-ZPass.

title: One dead after crash in Tewksbury
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: One person died following a head-on collision involving two vehicles on Andover Street in Tewksbury.
Both drivers were treated on-scene and transported to hospitals; one of them succumbed to injuries.
Andover Street was closed for several hours Sunday night for a crash reconstruction investigation.
